behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is in request construction logic — the module assembles the wrong payload before ever touching the network. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network required. LLM-DEP: We are testing what we send, not what the model returns; no non-determinism involved. INTEGRATION-CHECK Down-test: we can call the classify function with a mocked API client, capture the call arguments, and assert the labels list matches the taxonomy — one function boundary, no cross-module wiring needed.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the classifier with a known taxonomy list. Mock the external API client. Call classify(document). Capture the arguments passed to the mocked API call. Assert that the labels field in the captured request equals the full taxonomy list exactly.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns 'unknown' instead of forwarding the unexpected value.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is in the response-validation/mapping logic inside the module, not at a cross-module boundary. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network needed. LLM-DEP: We control the return value by mocking the API — deterministic. INTEGRATION-CHECK Down-test: mock the API to return an out-of-taxonomy string, call classify(), assert return value is 'unknown'. One function boundary, no wiring required.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API client to return a label string that is not in the configured taxonomy (e.g., 'marketing'). Call classify(document). Assert the return value is exactly 'unknown'. Optionally parametrize over several invalid labels including empty string and None to cover edge variants.

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE: If the API is mocked, repeated calls trivially return the same mocked value — the test always passes regardless of real model behavior. LLM-DEP: The claim is about consistency of a real AI model's output. Running the same prompt 100 times against a production LLM can produce different labels (temperature, sampling). No deterministic in-process substitute can reveal this failure. The behavior's correctness depends entirely on what a real model produces, so Agentic is the minimum level that can catch the actual failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Call classify(same_document) N times (e.g., 5–10) against the real external AI API with no mocking. Collect all returned labels. Assert all elements of the result list are identical. Flag any variance as a test failure, and record the distribution to surface flakiness rather than ignoring it.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Budget enforcement typically requires at least two cooperating modules — a token counter and a request builder — to be wired together. A failure where the token counter and the budget-check threshold live in separate modules and are connected incorrectly would not be caught by testing either module in isolation. INTEGRATION-CHECK Down-test: a budget check embedded in a single function could be caught at Unit; however, the common failure mode is the integration between token-counting utilities and request-assembly logic. Up-test: token counts for a given prompt are deterministic, so real API execution is not required. In-process module wiring is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Configure the classifier with a small, explicit token budget. Provide a document whose raw token count would exceed the budget without enforcement. Wire together the real token-counting module and the real request-building module (no API mock needed for request construction). Assert that either (a) the assembled request's token count is within budget, or (b) the classifier raises a budget-exceeded error before calling the API. Use a real token-counting library (e.g., tiktoken) rather than a stub.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real token counter and real request builder wired together, which is Integration. If both live in the same function, Unit would suffice — but the integration wiring is the more likely failure surface.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The retry/backoff logic lives inside the module itself. The failure (propagating a rate-limit error instead of retrying) is observable by mocking the API to raise a rate-limit exception. REAL-INFRA: No real network or OS behavior needed; time.sleep can be mocked. LLM-DEP: No model output involved. INTEGRATION-CHECK Down-test: mock the API client so the first call raises a rate-limit error and the second returns a valid label; call classify(); assert the valid label is returned and no error is raised — one function boundary, no cross-module wiring required.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API client so it raises a rate-limit exception on the first call and returns a valid taxonomy label on the second. Also mock time.sleep (or equivalent) to avoid real delays. Call classify(document). Assert the return value is the valid label (not an error or exception). Assert time.sleep was called at least once with a non-negative duration, confirming the module backed off before retrying.
